hero.title: Anticipate customs barriers. Cross borders flawlessly.
hero.subtitle: In your import and export operations; eliminate customs declaration errors, HS code discrepancies, and regulatory risks with data before execution. Leave no room for chance in global trade.
hero.trustline: Engineered for the global trade operations of European and international enterprises.

problem.title: Three strategic risks overlooked in global trade
problem.subtitle: As companies integrate into global markets, they lose capital and time at these three critical junctures. We eliminate these risks with data-driven precision before your customs declarations are filed.

card1.title: Incorrect Declarations & Customs Penalties
card1.body: Misclassified HS codes, missing documentation, or categorical errors cost companies heavily in penalties and supply chain delays every year. Pre-auditing processes increases upfront costs by 2%, while retroactive corrections increase them by 30%.

card2.title: CBAM, ESG & Regulatory Barriers
card2.body: Next-generation regulations like the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) and ESG directives require strict supply chain accountability. Architect your compliance strategy in advance to secure your market operations.

card3.title: Data-Blind Market Selection
card3.body: Most enterprises rely on intuition for market prioritization. When logistics, tariffs, and demand curves are calculated holistically, 40% of strategic decisions change entirely.

methodology.title: From Raw Data to Seamless Operations.
methodology.subtitle: A 360-degree intelligence ecosystem covering not just theoretical analysis, but actual field realities—from customs regimes to logistics corridor simulations.

step1.title: 13-Phase Intelligence Cycle
step1.body: A proprietary analysis process driven by TAM/SAM/SOM market sizing, regulatory mapping, and legal compliance scanning.

step2.title: Field & Logistics Validation
step2.body: Cost validation with real-time quotes from our portfolio of customs and logistics partners in target markets.

step3.title: Armored Data Protocol
step3.body: Military-grade (AES-256) encryption of client data, with permanent digital destruction (DoD 5220.22-M standards) on day 15 of the project.

step4.title: Decision Support Mechanism
step4.body: Low, medium, and high-risk scenario stress-testing, accompanied by active operational mentoring during the first 3 shipments.

caseStudies.title: Strategic Case Studies
caseStudies.subtitle: Concrete field cases demonstrating how operational barriers in global markets are overcome with the DutyInsight methodology.
caseStudies.footnote: Note: Raw data used in these case studies has been permanently destroyed post-delivery in accordance with our strict Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs). All presented metrics are fully anonymized.

scenarioA.title: European Market Entry & Corporate Scaling
scenarioA.challenge: Uncertainty regarding local legal compliance and operational pressure on profitability during the brand's EU expansion.
scenarioA.approach: Deployed the Strategy Intelligence Engine to execute a 13-phase analysis; simulating optimal legal structures and conducting channel-based (Marketplace vs. B2B) profitability stress tests.
scenarioA.outcome: Delivered 20-40% optimized profit margins and a Ready-to-Scale operational infrastructure prior to customs engagement.

scenarioB.title: Regulatory Barriers & Technical Intelligence
scenarioB.challenge: Category ambiguity of product components under the EU Biocidal Products Regulation and the risk of customs rejection.
scenarioB.approach: Conducted technical dossier analysis via the Export Entry Intelligence Engine. Mapped the customs duty environment by constructing a Certification Gap Matrix.
scenarioB.outcome: Generated 'Readiness Scores' for three target markets, preventing operational bottlenecks and financial losses before they occurred.

scenarioC.title: Ecosystem Modeling & Rollout Strategy
scenarioC.challenge: Uncertainty in support load and trust thresholds due to a lack of density analysis for geographically dispersed providers.
scenarioC.approach: Mapped the market into 5 main clusters using Numerical Ecosystem Analysis and analytically simulated 3 different rollout scenarios.
scenarioC.outcome: Identified a 4.6x risk multiplier in support load and structured a controlled growth plan optimizing the trust threshold.

reports.title: Trade Intelligence Reports
reports.subtitle: Import and Export Intelligence Reports are offered in flexible packages based on your catalog size. The Strategy Intelligence Engine is a 13-phase, custom-built decision support mechanism for your enterprise.

mail.subject: DutyInsight - Inquiry regarding {{plan}}

Hello DutyInsight team,

We would like to get more detailed information about your {{plan}} package and initiate the process.

Looking forward to your response.

Best regards,

reports.footnote: DutyInsight operations are built on a foundation of 'Maximum Confidentiality'. All data is processed in offline environments isolated from internet access.

strategy.title: Decode the unseen dynamics of the market.
strategy.subtitle: Strategy Intelligence is a deep, one-time market analysis and investment advisory service tailored specifically for your firm's SaaS or physical products.

feature1.title: Data-Driven Market Prioritization
feature1.body: Which 3 markets actually drive profit? An entry model weighted by customs tariffs, logistics costs, and demand data.

feature2.title: Cross-Border Competitor Mapping
feature2.body: Which markets are your competitors entering, and with what pricing strategy? Clear analysis of production hubs and target markets.

feature3.title: Regulatory & Compliance Roadmap
feature3.body: The impact of next-generation regulations like CBAM, ESG, and EU IOSS on your sector for the 2026-2028 period, complete with a preparation calendar.

feature4.title: Board of Directors Briefing
feature4.body: A 30-50 page premium report covering operational risks and opportunities, accompanied by a C-level management presentation.

strategy.ctaBody: We listen to your corporate objectives and collaboratively define the scope of your bespoke strategy.

hub.title: Two Hubs. Borderless Trade.
hub.subtitle: We manage operational flawlessness from Istanbul, and your European vision from Prague.

istanbul.title: Customs & Compliance
istanbul.description: We prevent declaration errors in your import and export processes, mitigating local regulatory risks to zero with data-driven HS code audits.

prague.title: European Market Access
prague.description: We measure the European market volume, ensure your ESG/CBAM compliance, and architect your investment strategy to international standards.
prague.feature2: Digital and Physical Product Growth Strategy

getStarted.subject: DutyInsight - Partnership Inquiry
getStarted.body: Hello DutyInsight Team, I have reviewed your platform and would like to request information regarding your services.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At DutyInsight, we place the utmost importance on protecting the data of corporate clients utilizing our services. This Privacy Policy has been prepared in accordance with the Turkish Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698 ("KVKK"), the European Union General Data Protection Regulation ("GDPR"), and related legislation.

section1.title: 1. Data Controller and Definitions
section1.body: In its capacity as Data Controller, DutyInsight ("Company") acts as the data controller under KVKK and as the "data controller" under GDPR. "Client" refers to the legal entity benefiting from DutyInsight services for commercial purposes; "User" refers to the natural person accessing the Platform on behalf of the Client.

section2.title: 2. Categories of Processed Data
section2.body: The following data categories are processed via the Platform: Identity and Contact Data, Client Company Data (Commercial title, Tax ID), Operational Trade Data (HS codes, routes), Technical Data (IP, logs), and Financial Data (Billing history).

section3.title: 3. Purposes and Legal Bases for Processing
section3.body: Personal data is processed within the scope of KVKK Art. 5/2 and GDPR Art. 6 for the following purposes: Establishment and performance of the contract, fulfillment of legal obligations, ensuring Platform security, and in line with legitimate interests.

section4.title: 4. Data Security and Encryption
section4.body: All data is protected with the TLS 1.3 protocol during transit and the AES-256 encryption algorithm during storage. Access is restricted via role-based access control (RBAC), and our systems comply with ISO/IEC 27001 standards.

section5.title: 5. Data Transfer
section5.body: Data may be shared with group companies, GDPR-compliant cloud providers, or public institutions in the event of a legal obligation, strictly to the extent necessary for service performance. Cross-border transfers are based on Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs).

section6.title: 6. Rights of the Data Subject
section6.body: Under KVKK Art. 11 and GDPR Arts. 15-22, you possess the right to: Learn whether your data is processed, request access, rectification, erasure (right to be forgotten), and data portability.

section7.title: 7. Retention Periods
section7.body: Data is retained only for the duration required by the processing purpose. Client contract data is preserved for 10 years, and financial records for 5 years. Upon expiration, data is anonymized or permanently destroyed.

section8.title: 8. Cookies
section8.body: The Platform utilizes cookies for functional and analytical purposes. Please review our Cookie Policy for detailed information.

section9.title: 9. Policy Updates
section9.body: The Company reserves the right to revise this Policy in accordance with legislative changes. Significant updates will be communicated via email.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: To exercise your rights or for any inquiries, you may submit a written request to info@dutyinsight.com. Applications will be addressed within 30 days.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These Terms of Use govern the legal conditions regarding the use of the platform and subscription services provided by DutyInsight. By accessing the Platform, you are deemed to have accepted these Terms.

section1.title: 1. Definition of Service
section1.body: Services consist of trade intelligence reports, pre-customs risk analyses, CBAM/ESG compliance assessments, and strategic advisory deliverables. Services are exclusively designed for professional B2B utilization.

section2.title: 2. Account Authorization
section2.body: The Client warrants the accuracy of the information provided. Maintaining the confidentiality of account credentials is the Client's responsibility, encompassing an obligation to notify the Company in the event of unauthorized access.

section3.title: 3. Subscription and Usage
section3.body: Report quotas within a package are valid for 12 months. Unused quotas are non-refundable. The right of withdrawal expires upon the delivery of the digital service.

disclaimer.title: 4. Disclaimer — Legal Nature
disclaimer.body: Reports are of a strategic advisory nature; they do not substitute official customs declarations, legal opinions, or binding official decisions. DutyInsight cannot be held liable for penalties imposed by customs authorities.

liability.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
liability.body: The Company's total liability is strictly limited to the amount paid for the service subject to the breach within the preceding 12 months. Indirect damages, such as loss of profit or reputational harm, are excluded.

section6.title: 6. Data Accuracy
section6.body: Reports rely on official third-party sources (e.g., Eurostat, UN Comtrade). The Company cannot be held responsible for errors or delays originating from these sources.

section7.title: 7. Client Obligations
section7.body: Information may only be utilized for internal commercial activities. Automated data extraction (scraping), reverse engineering, or unauthorized access attempts are strictly prohibited.

ip.title: 8. Intellectual Property
ip.body: All reports, analytical models, and content generated within the scope of the Services are the exclusive property of DutyInsight and are protected by copyright law.

section9.title: 9. Termination
section9.body: The Company reserves the right to suspend Services or unilaterally terminate the contract in the event of non-payment or breach of these terms.

section10.title: 10. Force Majeure
section10.body: The Company's obligations are suspended during events beyond reasonable control, such as natural disasters, cyber-attacks, or actions by public authorities.

section11.title: 11. Governing Law
section11.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Türkiye. The Central Courts of Istanbul (Çağlayan) have exclusive jurisdiction over the resolution of disputes.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For questions and notifications, you may contact info@dutyinsight.com.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This Policy has been prepared to inform you about the tracking technologies utilized on dutyinsight.com.

section1.title: 1. What is a Cookie?
section1.body: Cookies are small text files placed on your device via your browser to recognize you on subsequent visits and remember your preferences.

section2.title: 2. Cookie Types
section2.body: DutyInsight uses two types of cookies on the Platform: Strictly necessary cookies (operational) and analytical cookies (performance measurement).

essential.title: 3. Strictly Necessary Cookies
essential.body: Used to provide session management, security, and language preferences. They are mandatory for the Platform to function and cannot be disabled.

analytics.title: 4. Analytical Cookies
analytics.body: Collect anonymized data to measure user experience. They enable us to identify which pages attract attention and detect technical issues.

section5.title: 5. Third-Party Cookies
section5.body: Cookies belonging to verified, GDPR-compliant analytical service providers (e.g., Vercel Speed Insights) may be used to enhance service quality.

section6.title: 6. Management
section6.body: You can manage, block, or delete your cookie preferences at any time via the panel on the Platform or through your browser settings.

section7.title: 7. Retention Periods
section7.body: Session cookies are deleted when the browser is closed. Analytical cookies are stored for a maximum of 13 months, after which your consent is requested again.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For information regarding updates to the cookie policy, you may contact info@dutyinsight.com.

cookieBanner.message: DutyInsight uses strictly necessary cookies for the proper functioning of the Platform and analytical cookies to enhance the user experience. You can manage your preferences.
